methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Multiply Labs is a Series A-stage biotech company founded by Fred Parietti and Alice Melocchi that has raised $41M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including recent-stage comparables like Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B Series B), Neko Health ($1.8B Series B), and Asimov ($800M Series B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $85M total-raised proxy as a base and applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to next stage, implying $170M. Peer-Median analysis across 6 same-sector, adjacent-stage companies yields a median valuation of $805M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the public sector median P/S is 5.8x, but Multiply Labs' revenue is not on file, as is typical for private-stage companies.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $144M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $488M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around revenue scale and capital efficiency relative to the peer set. The Stage-Progression method anchors conservatively to near-term milestones; Peer-Median reflects what comparable companies have achieved at adjacent stages but encompasses substantial variance in business model and market timing.
